subject_id: DOLCE-driven:Role (Masolo et al. 2004, not core DOLCE)
comment: Corrected (2026-07-06, full-corpus audit) from `exact-equivalence-candidate`: both are anti-rigid, foundation/dependence-grounded, relationally-conditioned types; UFO explicitly continues this OntoClean-adjacent lineage, and the informal characterization matches unusually closely. But the two frameworks still differ in formal apparatus (DOLCE-driven: `CF`-classification of a `Concept`; UFO: anti-rigid sortal specializing a Kind) — the same kind of divergence `mapping-002`/`mapping-003` already use to withhold `exact-equivalence-candidate` for BFO against these same two sources. Applying that standard consistently: `close-match`, not `exact-equivalence-candidate`. `high` confidence in `close-match`, higher than `mapping-002`/`003`'s `medium`: both sources are independently fetched and directly verified (2026-07-01), and the informal-level convergence here is textually closer than either BFO comparison — a confidently-established, not open, finding.

Both are dependent, context-grounded, non-defining characterizations of a bearer. Not `exact-equivalence-candidate` because BFO's structural definition and the anti-rigidity/foundation meta-property definition are formally different apparatus (`note-002`), and the object here is extension literature, not core DOLCE (now independently verified against `xwkont:ref:masolo-vieu-2004`, though this does not change the formal-apparatus divergence).
Same confirmed metaphysical divergence as `quality.md` mapping-004: BFO's Role is a realizable entity dependent on a bearer; SUMO's SocialRole is an abstract entity.

Both are `Dependent` roles specifically grounded in a Relator/relator-like mediator (`role_of` some `Relator` in GFO; UFO's Role held "in the scope of" a Relator), directly paralleling `relation.md`'s GFO/UFO Relator convergence at the Role-specific level (`note-005`). `medium-high`, not `high`, for a genuine sourcing-completeness reason: GFO's own file supplies `Relational_role` with only structural axioms, no prose theory to compare against UFO's elaborated account — a documentation gap in the primary source, not a confirmed substantive divergence.

A confirmed metaphysical divergence within the trope/dependent-particular camp itself, not just against SUMO: BFO's Role is a dependent Realizable Entity, while GFO's `Social_role` is explicitly `Independent` (`owl:disjointWith` `Dependent`) — the only source in this crosswalk to treat any role subtype as independent of a bearer (`note-005`).

Both are context-dependent, non-abstract role treatments illustrated with directly comparable examples (BFO: "student role... university"; YAMATO: "teacher role... school") (`note-006`). Not `high` because YAMATO's own text discloses its role theory is not fully elaborated in this paper — deferred to Mizoguchi (2007), not independently fetched.

No TUpper correspondence identified; "role" appears only informally inside `participates_in`'s prose gloss, never reified as a class — consistent with `quality.md`'s and `relation.md`'s TUpper findings across this entire dependent-particular family.

GUM's `RolePlaying` is a relation type (subclass of `Symbolization`), not an object/entity type — the same pattern already found for GUM's Information-Artifact and Relation analogs (`note-007`). Not confidently placed in any existing category.
